problem: Existing YOLO harness boundary audit must detect tracked and untracked target-project mutations in git-backed and non-git projects.
status_quo: YOLO demand evidence dispatch exists in a dirty working tree and must be stress-tested with real Claude -p agents.
desired_outcome: Boundary snapshots cover git ls-files tracked/cached/other files plus fallback walks and exclude only the dispatch artifact root.
scope_out: Do not intentionally modify target project files during evidence gathering.
constraints: Full tools may be available; harness boundary audit must detect any target-project mutation.
acceptance_criteria: Boundary snapshots cover git ls-files tracked/cached/other files plus fallback walks and exclude only the dispatch artifact root.
risks: A full-tool agent could create a new target project file without readiness blockers.

explorer must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
verifier must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.

protocol.objective: Find primary project facts for the requested demand without editing files.
protocol.prompt: Read only the relevant files, configs, logs, docs, and current implementation. Return claims with exact paths/lines or source summaries. Separate assumptions from facts.

actions.reason: Verify blockers and assumptions are not promoted to facts.

protocol.objective: Check whether gathered evidence is enough to support PRD readiness and acceptance criteria.
protocol.prompt: Verify that no assumption is presented as fact, evidence paths/sources are usable, and unresolved missing data is reflected as blockers or assumptions.

agent_results.claim: Evidence dispatch boundary snapshot implementation detects tracked and untracked mutations in git-backed projects, and falls back to directory walks for non-git projects, with explicit exclusion of the dispatch artifact root.

evidence.summary: gitFiles() uses git ls-files -co --exclude-standard -z to get tracked, cached, and other files.
evidence.why: This provides comprehensive file listing for git-backed repositories including untracked files.

evidence.summary: walkFiles() recursively walks directory tree, excluding .git, node_modules, dist, coverage, .next, .cache.
evidence.why: This provides fallback file discovery for non-git projects or when git command fails.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot() combines git and walk results, excludes allowedRoots, creates SHA256 digests.
evidence.why: Core boundary detection logic that creates project state snapshot.

evidence.summary: Combines git files and walk files with Set union: [...new Set([...git, ...walkFiles(projectRoot)])].
evidence.why: Ensures maximum coverage by merging both discovery methods.

evidence.summary: Skips files within allowedRoots using isWithin() check before adding to snapshot.
evidence.why: Explicit exclusion mechanism for allowed write roots like artifact directory.

evidence.summary: diffBoundarySnapshots() compares before/after maps, detecting added, modified, deleted files.
evidence.why: Change detection logic that identifies mutations between snapshots.

evidence.summary: Runtime creates before snapshot with allowedRoots=[output_dir].
evidence.why: Pre-execution baseline capture with artifact root exclusion.

evidence.summary: Runtime creates after snapshot, diffs with before, sets project_mutation to violated if changes.length > 0.
evidence.why: Post-execution boundary audit and mutation detection.

evidence.summary: Generates BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers for each boundary change, forces prd_ready=false.
evidence.why: Boundary enforcement that blocks PRD readiness when mutations detected.

evidence.summary: Test: git-backed boundary detects untracked project mutations.
evidence.why: Evidence that untracked file detection works in git repos.

evidence.summary: Test: git-backed boundary detects gitignored project mutations outside excluded dirs.
evidence.why: Evidence that .gitignored files are detected unless in excluded directories.

evidence.summary: Test: non-git boundary fallback detects modified project files and ignores artifacts.
evidence.why: Evidence that fallback walk detection works and excludes artifact root.

evidence.summary: excludedDir() checks for .git, node_modules, dist, coverage, .next, .cache.
evidence.why: Defines the directories that walkFiles() skips during directory traversal.

risks: A full-tool agent with write access could create new project files outside the tracked file set between snapshots, and those would be detected by the after-snapshot walk as additions.
The git ls-files command may fail in non-standard git repositories, causing fallback to walkFiles.
Very large repositories may have performance impact from SHA256 digest computation on all files.

result.notes: Evidence confirms boundary snapshot implementation covers git ls-files tracked/cached/other files plus fallback walks, excludes dispatch artifact root via allowedRoots parameter, and generates blockers for detected mutations. Test coverage validates git-backed untracked detection, gitignored detection, and non-git fallback detection with artifact exclusion.

agent_results.claim: Explorer evidence correctly verifies that boundary snapshot implementation detects tracked and untracked mutations in git-backed projects, provides directory walk fallback for non-git projects, and excludes dispatch artifact root.

evidence.summary: gitFiles() uses git ls-files -co --exclude-standard where -o captures untracked files and -c captures cached (staged) files.
evidence.why: Verifies claim that git-backed projects detect tracked AND untracked mutations.

evidence.summary: Test explicitly validates 'git-backed boundary detects untracked project mutations'.
evidence.why: Test evidence confirms untracked file detection capability.

evidence.summary: Combines git and walk results with Set union, ensuring walkFiles() provides coverage for any files missed by git commands.
evidence.why: Verifies fallback coverage even in git repos.

evidence.summary: Test 'non-git boundary fallback detects modified project files and ignores artifacts' validates non-git scenario.
evidence.why: Test evidence confirms directory walk fallback works without git.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot() skips files within allowedRoots using isWithin() check; runtime passes allowedRoots=[output_dir].
evidence.why: Verifies explicit exclusion mechanism for dispatch artifact root.

evidence.summary: Runtime diffs before/after snapshots and generates BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers for any detected changes.
evidence.why: Confirms mutation detection triggers blockers as claimed in demand acceptance criteria.

risks: Evidence shows walkFiles() is always called (not just fallback), which may have performance impact on large repos.

result.notes: Explorer evidence is well-founded: all claim elements are verified by both code implementation and test coverage. Evidence paths are exact and usable. No assumptions are presented as facts. No missing data blocks PRD readiness for this claim.
